preamble: The transformation of the voiceless alveolar fricative /s/ into the diphthong /au/ is an unusual phonetic shift, primarily attested as a specific morphophonological rule within the historical grammar of Sanskrit.
phoneticEffects: This shift involves the vocalization of a stem-final /s/ consonant into a diphthong, representing a complete change in manner and place of articulation from a fricative to a vowel

examples.note: The stem-final /s/ changes to /au/ in the nominative singular of the pronoun 'adas' ('that'), resulting in 'asau'. This is a specific rule in Pāṇini's grammar.

sources: Siddha and Asiddha in Pāṇinian Phonology - “Current Approaches to Phonological Theory” in “Current Approaches to Phonogical Theory” | Open Indiana | Indiana University Press.
